sentences.text: Yo.
What's going on, Justin?
Too much, how you doing?
Oh, good.
Never gonna complain on a 4-day work week.
I want my video's all fucked up.
Huh.
Hang on a second.
Oh, my… can you hear me?
I can hear ya.
My Zoom's, like, spinning, I don't know.
My video's frozen, it won't let me click anything.
Oh, no.
Yeah, your sea were blurry.
Yeah, hang on, I don't know, it's like… like a rainbow pinwheel spinning.
I, like, can't leave to rejoin either.
Oh, no, the, the Mac.
The Mack spin wheel.
That's what it's doing, yeah.
I'll give it a second.
Let's see…
It won't let me click anything, even to, like, The hell?
What's… what's the, what's the command quit button?
What is it?
Command…
That's what I'm trying, yeah.
Command, Option, delete.
I used to be Ctrl-Alt-Delete on the old computers, let me show you what it is.
Why do I think it's Command-Q?
I don't know if that's right.
Command, Option, Escape.
Command, Option Escape.
Alright, that was it.
Yep, I'm gonna quit, I'll be right back.
Okay.
made me do an update, so maybe that's… there we go, nice.
There we go.
Nice.
It made me do an update whenever I closed it, so maybe that was kind of, like, in between an update or something.
That was weird.
Yeah.
I'm like, yeah, you could always miss this one, but the next one's important.
Yeah, it'll be interesting to see.
I mean, we… a couple of those guys, they trialed it last year, so we'll probably do, like, a quick overview of…
Just market surveying, and then… Yeah, I mean, what's weird about, like, Explore is, like, they're… they're…
pretty much developers.
I don't know if they'd want to use it for, like, new business or whatever, so… Yeah.
What sucks, too, is this will probably be… if we do get them, it'll be, like, a $2,000 a month.
deal.
It'll never be a per unit, per month model.
Yeah.
It would be nice to get them, though, just to get it out of the way.
Yeah, that's, like, what Corey and I were kind of talking about, was like.
It won't even be anything big.
Right.
Okay, NTS, this should be… A bunch of regionals?
Okay.
They do everything manual and co-star.
So, we've met with, a colleague of theirs, Dustin, or… yeah, I think…
Yeah, back in June, I think, right?
Yes.
Yeah.
And so today, Stephanie Burns is the main contact.
She's a VP.
Okay.
I'll let her in.
Good afternoon.
Bye!
Hi, Stephanie, how are you?
Ben, how are you doing?
I'm doing good.
I was telling Justin, there's never a complaint on my end on a 4-day work week.
Yeah.
That's good.
Yeah, I can see we got the gang all here.
Do you want to do quick, intros real quick?
Sure.
I'm Stephanie, I'm with NTS, I'm over the Tennessee and Texas portfolio.
I'm Kendra Ware, I'm over in Indianapolis.
I'm Allison Zimmerman, and I'm over Kentucky.
Allie O'Grady, and I'm over Florida and Virginia.
Awesome, nice to meet you all.
I'm Hunter, I'm a sales rep over here, and then my colleague Justin, he is a sales rep as well.
So, glad to get in front of the group.

We met with your colleague Dustin earlier this year, so a little bit familiar with, kind of, the processes in place, but, I'll definitely have you, kind of, give us a recap on that in case anything has changed, but today's call should be pretty casual, back-and-forth conversation around, rent comp and market, data analysis.

Figure out the processes in place, we'll give you the full scope as to what we can do, as far as ApartmentIQ goes, walk you through the platform, and then at the end of the call, just collectively come together and give us your thoughts, feedback, and if you think this could, potentially help kind of streamline and automate your process.
How's that sound?
Great, great.
Awesome.
So it'd be beneficial for Justin and myself to just get a recap as, the processes in place goes.
I think earlier, correct me if I'm wrong, everything's manual, and then I think you guys also utilize CoStar.
Yeah, and I also use ApartmentData.
com, because they're based… they're only in search markets, so they're in Nashville and Dallas, so… use them for that.
Okay.
And then, from, like, the manual, kind of, market data collection, I'm assuming, like, are the regionals in charge of that?
Site teams?
What's the frequency?
really not doing it.
It's really pointless at this point, given just the nature of the, you know, class action lawsuit.

Most companies now are just not, you know, providing that information when you call them, so… we've become more heavily dependent on CoStar and, you know, any other market data reports that we can kind of seek out.
Yeah.
And, who all internally is, utilizing CoStar?
All of us.
All of us, yeah.
Pretty much.
In our commercial division as well.
We have a commercial side as well, and they use CoStar.
Okay.
Are you guys in there, like, daily, weekly?
What's the… what's the cadence on that?

Yeah, we… I mean, I think it just… it ebbs and flows, depending on, you know, especially if you have a property that is underperforming, I think I'm definitely in there more frequently, and we're in… currently in budget season, so definitely… Reviewing that more frequently as we're trying to forecast, you know, revenue growth and things like that.
Sure.
Are there… have there been any talks of trying to find other data providers outside of CoStar, or what do you like about it, what do you don't like about it?
Yeah, I think that's kind of what, spearheaded this conversation.
So I, reached out to some colleagues, in Nashville, asking them how they're getting their data, and I know Willowbridge, that use ApartmentIQ, and That's basically what prompted me to reach out.
Just, I think, sometimes CoStar is not always as accurate as we'd like it to be, and… Just understanding the different platforms and seeing what would potentially be a good fit.
Sure.
You will probably not be surprised to hear that, Justin and I pretty much hear that on every call, on how Coast Guard's not reliable.
We've heard every explanation from A to Z, so you're not alone there.
Good news is I definitely think we could help you out.
And, yeah, Willowbridge does… they utilize us.
Justin and I were a part of that sales cycle with them, so very familiar with, their entire team over there, and, good partner of ours.
But, as far as ApartmentIQ goes, what all information do they tell you that we can provide?
That way, I can know where to fill in the gaps.
I mean, we didn't… we had a really brief conversation, to be honest.
We didn't do a deep dive, so I think it would be helpful just to go through everything.
Yeah, for sure.
So, We've been around the last 5 years.
All of the data within the platform is gonna be 100% publicly available information.
So, it's information that a renter could go out and find, or you and I could go out and find, except for we just automate it for you.
So when you log into the platform, you're looking at live data, as of today.
So there's 3 functionalities, one of which is for your owned or managed assets.
So when you log in, you're looking at a live data feed for your properties and each specific comp set that you're tracking.
That way, you can see where your units and floor plans are positioned within each specific comp set.
And the nice thing about that is you can aggregate the data back, over the last 5 years.
Depending on who's looking at it, what sort of picture you're trying to paint, you could do today, week over week, month over month, quarter over quarter, etc, all the way back to 5 years.
Second functionality is great for acquisitions or new development.
All you need is the target property name or address, and you can spin up these custom live market surveys on the fly.
Takes about 3 seconds, and for both of those functionalities, you and your teams are in complete control of your comp set, so you can edit those as often as you'd like.
I think the cap is maybe, like, 40 comps per asset.
We don't have anybody ever use that many, but just know that you can go up that high.
Last functionality is going to be very similar to CoStar.
It's all market, sub-market level information.

You can do different mile radiuses, boundaries, you could drill down to specific neighborhoods, and then we do give you, in that report all new supply data, the different stages of construction pipeline that it's in, and then demographic information, to help you better understand the renter profile of that given market or sub-market.
So we used to just be a market survey tool to automate that, and then partnering with, you know, like, Willowbridge, Avenue 5, a handful of others.
They basically said, hey, look, we have our hands in so many different data providers, and we don't love any of them.
Could you just help us, like, piece together one so we could kind of just streamline all this into one platform?
And that's essentially what we built out.
Okay, cool.
So everything we'll show you today, just so you're aware, all the rent comp data comes directly from the online leasing section of the property websites.
So, we can essentially track, anyone in the States, as long as they have an online presence.
Doesn't have to be a customer of ours, they just need a website with online leasing.
That's it.
Oh, yeah, a quick question on that.
So, if there was a floor plan that wasn't available, or that was just waitlisted, like, our waitlisted floor plans don't have a price associated with it, so would that be represented in… that data.
If a unit's not available right now, it won't be, but what's nice is we can actually give you data on the last time it was.
So if you maybe had your 1A available 2 months ago, we can show you what your asking prices were then, we can show you how long those units sat on the market, what concession was attached to it.
Okay.

So, yeah, you can… you'll see here, but whenever you log into the platform, the data's updated as of this morning, but you can aggregate it over whatever timeframe you want, or you can actually pick a specific date and time and go back and view the data from that vantage point, too.
Oh, wow.
Yeah.
Got it.
Yeah.
How many units do you guys have in the portfolio?
Gosh.
I had, like, 5,000.
Cool.
And do you think this is something you'd give access to to site teams, or would it just be more so the regional level and up?
I'd say property manager and up, maybe.
I mean, I don't know… If there's, you know, costs associated with the number of users, or how that works.
No, yeah, that's kind of what I was asking.

What's nice here is we do offer unlimited users, so we can give access to whoever's relevant to each property, and what's nice is there are different permission levels, so if you want to give access to a site team, they can only see their property, or, you know, each of you can kind of see your different divisions.
So there's some customization in terms of, you know, different, different permission levels.
And what else is nice is that we have a full training team, so we can do group sessions, one-on-one sessions, ongoing training sessions as you get new employees.
So we do a nice job of making sure that whoever's looking at the data, you know, can access all the information that they want based off of their need or their role.
Okay.
So… But, yeah, I'll take you through it.
Like Hunter said, there's 3 sections.

We'll start on the market surveys portion here, but the way this is laid out is that each one of your properties would get their own individual property card, and then all of the data is based off of that property-specific comp set.
So.
Typically what happens whenever we implement a customer is that they'll provide us with a list of comps that they've been tracking, and we'll start there.

However, we do have a comp matching algorithm, so we can make suggestions or recommendations on who we think you might want to track or follow, but what's great is that your group can always add or remove a property to your comp set in the application anytime you'd like.
So, literally complete control of whoever you do track or follow.
If you click into a report.
Once you're in here, the very top, this is gonna be your main navigation.
So, each property does have 4 reports associated with it.
I'll take you through each one very high level.
Before I do, just some simple functionalities.
So, like I said, you log into here, the data's updated as of this morning, but with this drop-down, this is where you can pick and choose that, that time frame that you might care about.
We also have both PDF and Excel exports that can be downloaded anytime you'd like.
They can also be automated to be delivered to an inbox on whatever frequency or cadence you'd like.
So maybe you have some investors that want, you know, a monthly or quarterly report, you can automate that for them.
Down below here, all these metrics on the left, they are going to be sortable from high to low, low to high.
We also make it very easy to manipulate the data in the application, so you can look at different bedroom or bathroom combos, square footage ranges, rent price ranges.
If you'd like, you can even include or exclude renovated units, classic units, penthouses, and affordable units.
Do you guys do anything with BTR by chance?
No.
Okay.
Reason I was asking is because we have the largest definitive BTR database in the entire industry.
So, I know some of our customers are getting into that space, so it could be helpful.
Yeah.
Keep that in mind if that's… if you guys are.
And then lastly, in the top right here, you'll see the Edit Comp button.
This is actually where you'd go to add or remove properties to your comp set.
If you click in here, on the very right, you get a map view of the area.

So the blue pin would be your property, the white pins or property's already added to your comp set, and the black pins are properties that are in the area that you might want to consider adding as well.
It's as simple as clicking Add Comp, and all of the data plus the historicals are instantly added to your comp set.
Over here on the left, the major call-out in this list view is going to be our comp scoring.

So we do assign a score to every property in the vicinity of yours, and these scores are… they're essentially correlation scores, but a reference point in terms of how strong our system thinks a comp is in comparison to your property.
And those scores are based off of 5 factors.
So we take into account things like distance or proximity to your property.
We also look at vintage, total units, and then we do some pricing and amenity matching.
So those 5 factors spit out these scores.
I always say it's objectively or very directionally correct, but at the end of the day, you know your markets, you can pick and choose and add whoever you want.
In terms of the market survey, though, it's gonna be broken out into 5 sections.
So the first one here is Property Summary.
This is your high-level property data.
So we can show you things like how far each comp is away, who manages the property.
We can give you links to the Google reviews.
Down below here, we're gonna let you know if there is a rent special or concession tied to the property, or even specific floor plans or units in the property.
What's great is we can present concessions two different ways.
We can do it as a percentage of rent, or we can even just show you the value of it.
But then from there, we'll also show you, both gross and net rents at the property level.
If you keep scrolling, this is where we're going to give you those performance metrics.
So we can show you your occupancy, lease exposure percentages, we can hit on availability, and then we'll show you things like applications, cancels, and even leases.
Oh, wow.
Hmm.
And what I.
How do you track that?
Yeah, I'll explain.

I was gonna say, what's great about this is, like, the public data model in general is that any question about a performance metric, a calculation, you know, a concession, we can trace it all back to the source.
So it's not like we talked to a leasing agent, they said, oh, we're at 98%, then you jump on their website and they're offering a month free.
So, we can easily trace this back to the source, and what's great is you can audit all this data.
So, if you want to see the, the 25 units available in 1200 Broadway, you can click into here and see those 25 specific units.
Same idea with those units that have leased over the last 7 days.
You can see those 7 specific units that have leased over the last week.
And the way that we track all these performance metrics has to do with the fact that we are tracking unit-level data every single day.
So, for example here, I think the easiest way to explain to apps, cancels, and leases is that if a unit's available on the property website today, it's gonna fall under the available unit bucket.
If tomorrow that unit's gone from the property website, we're gonna assume that somebody put in an application on it.
And then if that unit doesn't reappear on the property website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled, then it becomes a lease.
So that's the process in terms of how you report these transactional performance metrics using publicly available data.
Credit.
Again, what's nice is, I mean, even talking about Willowbridge, whenever we rolled out their portfolio, 220,000 units, we were within 0.
5% of true occupancy for every one of their properties, including lease-ups.
And again… Yeah, if you want to audit the data, again, you can kind of look at the specific unit numbers and follow it through each stage.
You can kind of just watch it go from an available unit to an application to a lease.
But that's Section 2.
Section 3 is pricing and availability.
So this is very, like, high-level, quick and dirty, you know, where are my studios, where are my one-bedrooms, et cetera, positioned.
Inside of here, we give you every key metric to help understand that.

So maybe you're basing performance off of rent or price per foot, just then being able to easily sort this from high to low, or low to high, and see where your 1s or your twos or whatever might be stacked up.
We also show fees and deposits, so just making you aware of the ancillary fees and revenue opportunities out there.
And then lastly will be your in-unit and community-level amenities.
One final little call-out that I like here is you'll notice this first column on the left here, this comp average column.
This is like a nice us versus them or baseline, so if you just want to quickly, you know, see exactly where your property sits right now in terms of occupancy, hey, are we beating the baseline?
Or, you know, what's our leasing velocity like compared to everybody else?
I think that's just… just comparing these first two columns is an easy way to see where you're standing compared to the market.
The second report here is going to be floor plan level data.
Do you guys use a pricing tool?
Like, an automatic.
No, no, no, we price our own stuff annually.
So this might be a nice help for you.
So first of all, I'm gonna change the time frame, but first of all, what's nice is… We can give you the roll-up.
So if you just want to see what the average studio or average one-bedroom is in each property, we can give you that.

You know, at 805 Leah, their average studio is 508 feet, studios make up 20% of that property, and then you can look at their leased availability and rent trend data of whatever timeframe you care about as well.
What I like from a pricing conversation is if you toggle over here to floor plan, we'll actually show you each unique floor plan within a competitive set.
So if we look at the 1x1s, for example, the idea is just that, you know, not all 1-bedrooms compete with all other one-bedrooms.
Right.
The example here, we can see the smallest one starts at 516 feet at the alcove.
Where if I scroll all the way to the bottom, the largest one's gonna be, you know, closer to 1,000 square feet or 1,100 square feet here at the Harlow.
So we try to make it very easy to compare your one bedrooms or your floor plans off of like or similar sized floor plans.
And then, same idea, as you fold the data, you get unit mix, lease availability, and rent trend data over whatever time frame you do care about.
So, just a little bit of extra granularity at the floor plan level.
Like Hunter mentioned, we do have 5 years' worth of historical data that we give you access to right in the application, so you can pick and choose your time frame.
You can select your interval.
And then the data can be looked at at the property level or broken out by floor plan.
But inside of here, we're gonna give you rent trends, so both gross and net.

You can look at each individual's property's performance over that given time frame, or if you'd like, you can toggle over and compare your property's performance versus the median of the entire comp set.
So, you can understand rent trends.
We also give you concession history.
So again, you can look at concession history as a percentage or as a value.
We'll show you supply.
leasing velocity.
And also your occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.

So, it seems like post-pandemic we're back to some normalization in terms of seasonality, so this has been really helpful for our customers, especially in those markets where right now there's a lot of new supply coming on board.
Hell yeah.

Trying to manage those expiration curves is obviously tricky enough, and now you have all that supply hitting, and a lot of times it's hitting in non-peak leasing season, so just kind of take into account all of that, and, you know, it can be helpful.
But this is the market survey portion.
This is, again, comparing your property versus its 5, 7, 10 direct competitors.
Hunter mentioned part two of this, so it's called Research, but again, what this does is lets you create one of the reports that I just walked you through for any property in the country.
So we let you query our entire database, and it's a nice aid to help underwrite an acquisition.
So all you do is click New Report, you type in the name or the address of whatever property you care about, you find it down here in the list.
You click on it, and then in, like, 3 seconds, you have all of that data I just showed you.
From a development perspective, what's cool is, if you have a new development, we let you create a custom property.

So from here, you can type in… if you know… if you own the plot of land, you can type in the cross streets, or if you just want to develop in Scottsdale, Arizona, for example, you can type in Scottsdale, you can say, you know, we want to have 300 units, and then what's great is you can build out your unit mix.
So if you know you want to have 1, 2s, and 3s, the average square footages of each, and the rent price you're trying to achieve.
Based off of all these variables, we'll generate a market survey for you.

So, from here, I think it's helpful to, one, understand who your competitors are, and then, two, understand the rents that are achievable, as well as looking at those longer-term trends and seasonalities.
Understand their leasing velocity, their trajectories with occupancy and lease percentages.
So, nice to kind of underwrite a project, and then also, whenever it comes time to pre-leasing, you're kind of hitting the ground running.
So, that's part two.
Part 3, really excited about it.
Again, this is all market, sub-market data.
So from here, you can type in any city, town, neighborhood, zip code, county.
If you want, you can draw your own custom boundaries.
You can also type in a property name, property address, or, like, a point of interest, and get all this market data from a certain radius around that area.
But from here, Hunter and I are based out of Phoenix, so I Phoenix up, but, for Phoenix, for example, we're tracking 202,000 units in the Phoenix area.

We have every filtering option that you're gonna be used to, from CoStar, so vintage, total units, you can look at, you know, underperforming properties, or properties that have a certain price per foot for each bedroom, amenity mixes, or construction type.
So you can focus in on what you care about.
But inside of here, we're gonna give you 3 things.
So, one's gonna be rent comp data.
That's going to include historical rent growth, which can be looked at monthly, quarterly, yearly, both gross and net.
It's gonna include a 30-day snapshot of performance metrics and averages broken out by unit type.
And then we're also going to give you those same historicals that I just touched on a second ago as well.
So your rent trends, your supply by bed type.
You're leasing velocity by bed type.
And also those occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.
I'll let you know what's exciting about this, and I know one of you mentioned it, but we also have Forecasting, releasing here in about 3 weeks.
Okay.
stole, yeah, we actually stole one of the, the head, head economists from, from CoStar.
Excellent.
And he's been building this now for the past few months, so this is set to release, This month, so really excited for that.
A lot of positive feedback from our… a lot of our enterprise customers that had early access to it, like beta version access.
Yeah.
Looking at the map then, so there… you'll notice 3 different colored pins on the map.
So, black pins, they're gonna represent properties that exist.
The pink and yellow pins are going to represent properties under different stages of construction.
And if you put your mouse over those pins, we can give you details on the construction project.
On the left, we can show you how many units and properties make up each stage of construction.
As well as when they're slated to be delivered by quarter.
The ones that are kind of grayed out there, that's a historical look back.
The ones that are yellow or pink, there will be the… Forward-looking, the future-looking ones.
So, obviously, just being aware of the new supply in the area, how that's going to impact, you know, the operations of your existing property.
Lastly, here will be, your demographics.
So, population, education, income, employment data.
So… Lots of information in here.
I think the biggest difference is going to be the data quality.
So again, we're updating this every single day.
We have significantly better coverage than CoStar as well.
We had an enterprise group similar to Willowbridge, but they did an audit with CoStar versus us, and we were able to track 99% of all properties nationwide they wanted us to be able to track.
CoStar was at about 83%.

So better coverage, better data quality, and kind of like Hunter was saying, too, I think that we're going to give you everything all in one location, so rather than having to use 3-4 different sources, you'll be able to jump into here and understand, you know, exactly what's going on for everything multifamily.
So… I know you have the industrial arm of it, so CoStar is necessary there, but I think everything multifamily we can kind of solve with ApartmentIQ.
Cool.
That was a lot of information.
Any questions?

Well, I think you answered about the forecasting, that was one of the components that we really do like for CoStar, you know, especially when we're making budget assumptions on revenue growth and vacancy rates and things like that.
It helps us kind of back up what…
For sure.
Let me see if I can get access to this account.
Hang on a sec, let me see if I can show it to you.
It's in a… It's in a special… landing page, I'll see if I can… Give me one second.
No, I can't get access.
But it'll be out in the… it'll be out in a matter of weeks.
It's gonna be… just to share my screen again, it'll be built right into here, so it'll be a pretty slick view.
I mean, you know, you'll have, like, just a… kind of the bar chart showing forecasting.
Yeah.
And then you have a way to make, like, PDF reports if you wanted to put something together.
Oh, yeah.
like eating the co-star, like you said, yeah, like, you can curate that.
Yep, we got all kinds of PDF reports.
Yeah.
Yeah, lots of… yeah, lots of PDFs.
Our Excels are really dynamic, they, sorry, I usually have one open, but,
I'll show you what these look like in one second.
Sorry, my toolbar's in the way here.
And so this… the concessions are the ones that are showing up on their website.
Yeah, pop-ups, ribbons, banners, mouse-overs, images, etc.
If they're there, we can account for every single one of them.
And what's nice, too, is we can take those things down to the actual unit level.
So if it says, hey, you know, one month free off of units 1, 2, and 3, or 1 month free off of all 2 bedrooms, we can account for the level of specifics there.
Yeah.
Yeah, so this is our PDF.
You can see that it'll be property-specific, it's time-stamped.
And then down below, there's 6 different tabs.
That's gonna include that market survey, which I just walked you through.
Nice, clean, professional look to it.
You get both floor plan reports, you get the roll-up, or the averages of each bed type, then you get the breakdown of each individual unit, or each individual floor plan.
What I like is we have a visual market survey, so this data can be looked at the property level, we're broken out by floor plan.
Inside of here, it's like a dozen visuals breaking out every major KPI that you could be interested in as well.

So… Our goal with these things is pretty much just to let you download them and send them off, rather than having to kind of download them, package them yourself, make them look pretty, so… That's kind of, like, the goal with them.
But, yeah, that's… that's Apartment IQ.
Okay.
Does it integrate with Yardi, or, like, you just… it's literally just a platform you log into.
Yeah, I think that's the great part.

As far as, like, implementation goes, there's zero integrations, so you literally just give us a list of your properties, any preferred competitors, and you're set up and live and trained within 2 business weeks.
So it's a really seamless process.
Each, each of your users gets their own login, so, you know, your email address will be your login.
You can create your password, you have your own profile and everything, so it's pretty simple.
But yeah, how much?
So, we typically charge on, like, a per unit per month basis.
You get unlimited users that way, and I believe you said you're right around 5,000 units, so that would be, 55 cents per unit per month.
And that would just change as our unit counts change?
Like, or does that, like… the pricing change, like, because we… we hope to grow at some point.
I don't know that it'll be, like, anything crazy.
We're not going to be at the Willow Bridge, for example, with, like, 200,000 units or anything crazy, but…
Yeah, there's tiers, so as you grow, they can be, discounted.
And what's the contract like?
We… As far as, like, how long is it?
Yeah, we do, annual contracts, and then if you sell an asset or lose management, just a 30-day written notice of that asset.
Is that pretty standard for you?
I think so, yeah.
